pi_items.source: GeoGebra file format documentation (https://wiki.geogebra.org/en/Reference:File_Format): .ggb is the standard extension for GeoGebra Worksheet files
Direct comparison with task description: 'Plot these 5 points: A=(1, 2), B=(2, 3), C=(3, 5), D=(4, 4), E=(5, 7)' - both metadata and description specify identical coordinates
Task design: 5 polygons (squares) are required for visualizing residuals from 5 data points. Verifier checks for at least 5 polygons (line 158 in verifier.py: if num_polys >= 5)

GeoGebra official documentation: (1) FitLine Command (https://wiki.geogebra.org/en/FitLine_Command) - calculates regression line; (2) Polygon Command (https://wiki.geogebra.org/en/Polygon_Command) - creates polygons for residual squares; (3) Sum Command (https://wiki.geogebra.org/en/Sum_Command) - sums polygon areas. All commands verified to exist in GeoGebra.

privileged_info_summary: This is a synthetic task using test data. The task requires creating a GeoGebra worksheet (file extension .ggb) with 5 fixed data points: A=(1,2), B=(2,3), C=(3,5), D=(4,4), E=(5,7). The worksheet must contain at least 5 polygons to visualize squared residuals and use three GeoGebra commands: FitLine (for linear regression), Polygon (for residual squares), and Sum (for calculating total squared error). All metadata values are consistent with GeoGebra specifications and the task design requirements.
